pr:section.pr:title: Content Layer
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: General-purpose content-addressed references with hash integrity and three access modes.
pr:section.pr:p: Specifications refer to content beyond their own XML: test fixtures, configuration fragments, images, external documents. The content layer provides a uniform mechanism for these references. Each cnt:content node identifies content by a cryptographic hash, guaranteeing integrity regardless of where the content lives.

Three modes cover the spectrum of content locality. In referenced mode, a URL points to the canonical external location and the hash verifies its integrity on fetch. In embedded mode, a cnt:body child contains the content inline and the hash verifies the embedded copy. In both mode, the URL provides the canonical location while the body holds a local copy, with the hash binding both to the same content. An XSD 1.1 assertion enforces that at least one of URL or body is present, making empty content nodes structurally impossible.

The cnt:ref element enables inline references to content nodes from within prose, parallel to trm:ref for terminology and src:cite for sources. Its content attribute is an IDREF pointing to a cnt:content element's ID, and its mixed content provides display text.

The content layer is a foundation primitive. The testing layer builds on it to attach test fixtures and expected outputs to spec nodes. The source layer will eventually use it to embed or reference cited material with integrity guarantees. Any layer that needs to point at content beyond inline XML text can use cnt:content as its substrate.

org:concept.org:purpose: Understanding the content layer as a general-purpose, content-addressed reference mechanism with three access modes (referenced, embedded, both), hash integrity, and inline references via cnt:ref.

trm:term.trm:name: ContentType
trm:term.trm:definition: The XSD complexType that defines the structure of content nodes in the content layer: id, url, hash, optional media-type, and an optional body child for embedded content.

rel:relation.rel:note: The content layer adds content-addressed references as a semantic dimension within the layered architecture.
The ContentType complexType (id="type-content") is the schema definition for content nodes in the content layer.

spec:clayers.llm:node: Describes the content layer: general-purpose content-addressed references with hash integrity. Three modes: referenced (url + hash for external canonical locations), embedded (body + hash for inline content), or both (url for canonical, body for local copy). The cnt:ref element enables inline references to content nodes from prose, parallel to trm:ref and src:cite. Foundation primitive used by the testing layer for test fixtures and eventually by the source layer for cited material with integrity guarantees.
spec:clayers.llm:schema: The content schema provides content-addressed references with integrity guarantees. Each <cnt:content> node identifies content by hash with three modes: referenced (url + hash for external canonical locations), embedded (body + hash for inline content), or both. The hash attribute ensures integrity regardless of mode. The <cnt:ref> element enables inline references to content nodes from prose, parallel to trm:ref and src:cite. BodyType supports text, CDATA, base64 (via encoding attribute), and structured XML via xs:any.

A content-addressed reference node. The id makes it addressable in the spec graph. The hash attribute provides integrity verification. Three modes: referenced (url points to external canonical location), embedded (body child contains inline content), or both (url for canonical location, body for local copy). At least one of url or body must be present, enforced by XSD 1.1 assertion. The optional media-type classifies the content format.
spec:clayers.art:exempt: Conceptual documentation describing the content layer's purpose and design. No code implements this concept.

art:mapping.art:note: ContentType complexType: id, url, hash, media-type, optional body child.
